# services.teleport

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.teleport`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.teleport.diag.addr` | `string` | Metrics and diagnostics address. |
| `services.teleport.diag.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable endpoints for monitoring purposes. See <https://goteleport.com/docs/setup/admin/troubleshooting/#troubleshooting/> . |
| `services.teleport.diag.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Metrics and diagnostics port. |
| `services.teleport.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the Teleport service. |
| `services.teleport.insecure.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable starting teleport in insecure mode. This is dangerous! Sensitive information will be logged to console and certificates will not be verified. Proceed with caution! Teleport starts with disabled certificate validation on Proxy Service, validation still occurs on Auth Service . |
| `services.teleport.package` | `package` | The teleport package to use. |
| `services.teleport.settings` | `YAML 1.1 value` | Contents of the {file}`teleport.yaml` config file. The `--config` arguments will only be passed if this set is not empty. See <https://goteleport.com/docs/setup/reference/config/>. |
